rating.relevance_remarks: Content directly addresses Android environment configuration, aligning with the module topic. However, title says 'Configuration Of Android Environment' which is slightly awkward phrasing - 'Configuring' would be more natural.
rating.coverage_remarks: Covers basic steps (system requirements, JDK, Android Studio, SDK Manager, AVD) but omits critical details like environment variables/PATH configuration, Gradle basics, hardware acceleration for emulators, and command-line tools. Content appears cut off at 'Testin'.
rating.content_remarks: Accurate information but written in overly simplistic tutorial style rather than formal academic tone. MCQ is straightforward recall (2GB vs 4GB RAM) - too easy for Sem 6. Flashcard is basic definition-style. Lacks technical depth expected at B.Tech level.
rating.tier_remarks: Language is informal and tutorial-like ('Before you can build your first...', 'go to Tools > SDK Manager'). Should include formal definitions of SDK components, API levels, build tools versions, Gradle wrapper explanation. MCQs should test application (e.g., troubleshooting an emulator issue, calculating SDK disk space requirements). Expected ~200-250 lines but current content is too brief even accounting for truncation.
suggestions: Add formal definitions: What is Android SDK? API Level vs Platform version vs Build Tools version
Include environment variable configuration (ANDROID_HOME, PATH) with command-line instructions
Add section on hardware acceleration (Intel HAXM/AMD-V) for emulator performance
Create harder MCQs: scenario-based (emulator fails to start - diagnose), numerical (minimum disk space for SDK + emulator images)
Replace tutorial-style language with academic tone appropriate for engineering students
Add comparison table: JDK vs JRE vs Embedded JDK in Android Studio context

rating.relevance_remarks: Content directly addresses Android environment setup with appropriate tools (Android Studio, SDK). Sibling topics like Android OS intro are related but distinct.
rating.coverage_remarks: Covers core steps (system checks, JDK, Android Studio, SDK, AVD) but AVD section is incomplete. Missing Gradle setup, environment variables, or project structure details.
rating.quality_remarks: Accurate and logically structured, but AVD section abruptly ends. MCQs are correct but basic; explanations could be expanded (e.g., why JDK remains relevant with Kotlin).
rating.tier_remarks: Practical steps align with Standard tier, but lacks theoretical depth (e.g., SDK architecture, build process theory). MCQs are factual rather than application/numerical-focused.
suggestions: Add troubleshooting scenarios for common setup errors (e.g., SDK path issues) in MCQs.
Include a section on Gradle configuration and dependency management with code examples.
